topics.title: Wildfire smoke spreads across North America
topics.summary: Smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires is pushing unhealthy air into parts of the U.S. Midwest, Northeast and Mid-Atlantic, prompting warnings to stay indoors when air quality drops.
topics.discussionPrompt: Smoke drifting across borders makes weather feel less local. How would you plan a normal day when the air outside keeps changing?

topics.title: Mexico quake rattles a wide region
topics.summary: A magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck near the Mexico-Guatemala border, shaking communities from Chiapas to El Salvador. Officials reported no major damage, while aftershocks kept emergency crews alert.
topics.discussionPrompt: Earthquakes are one of those reminders that routine can change in seconds. What would you grab first if you had to step outside fast?

topics.title: Health officials trace lettuce outbreak
topics.summary: The FDA and CDC linked a five-state Cyclospora outbreak to shredded iceberg lettuce served at some Taco Bell locations. The chain removed the affected lettuce while investigators continue tracing supply paths.
topics.discussionPrompt: Food supply chains are invisible until something goes wrong. Does news like this change what you order, or do you wait for the all-clear?

topics.title: World Cup final buildup gets smoky
topics.summary: Spain and Argentina are preparing for the World Cup final as wildfire smoke, ticket prices and political side stories add extra attention around the tournament's closing weekend.
topics.discussionPrompt: A big final already has drama before kickoff. What outside issue would most change the mood of watching a huge game?

topics.title: NASA tests pieces of future Moon plans
topics.summary: NASA highlighted recent Artemis lander testing and new Mars rover science, keeping attention on the slow, technical work behind future Moon missions and planetary exploration.
topics.discussionPrompt: Space news can be less about one giant launch and more about patient testing. Do you find that exciting or painfully slow?
